channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.category: Damage - pole leaning / equipment detached from pole/facing the wrong direction

item.title: Overgrown Council Tree - needs pruning, 28th April
item.description:
This council‑owned tree beside my property has become very overgrown and has not been maintained for several years. It is positioned extremely close to the boundary (approx 3 feet away) and has now grown significantly taller than my house. I think it would be appropriate for the council to carry out an inspection and routine maintenance. As the tree is council‑owned, its condition and management fall within the council’s general duty of care, so an inspection would be helpful to keep it in a safe and manageable state. A photo has been provided to show the current situation. I would appreciate it if the relevant team could assess the tree and arrange the necessary trimming. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hammelton Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge tree overhanging road, pavement and neighbours., 28th April
item.description:
Tree needs maintenance to prevent an accident. Homeowner not been in residence for many years and uncontactable.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 3, Hill Brow, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrowing trees and bushes, 28th April

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waste being added to bin on property which results in waste not being collected, 28th April
item.description:
This is the bin at my property, placed in a location near the pavement but on the property within an enclosed area. Someone has filled this bin to the top with boxes and today I found toilet cleaning products dumped in the bin. There is hardly any room for my own waste and I have had a missed collection already. Is there a way to get this waste removed? I will be putting up a notice and a camera to deter further fly tipping. But how do I manage the waste that already there and the council won’t collect it?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Morgan Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pedestrian crossing button not working, 28th April
item.description:
Button does not trigger traffic lights to change
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 145, Hackney Road, Bethnal Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Van without plate number abandoned since 2 months, 28th April
item.description:
The van has no plate, probably no MOT and parked above the pavement. It also obstructs other cars for parking. This Van should be removed ASAP

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Close, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
